name: lean-ctx
install_doc: README get-started: `curl -fsSL https://leanctx.com/install.sh | sh` (or brew / `npm install -g lean-ctx-bin` / cargo), then `lean-ctx onboard` ('Connect your AI tools with one command (recommended)'). We pin the npm prebuilt binary `lean-ctx-bin@3.8.4` and run the official `lean-ctx onboard --yes` per run inside the sandbox HOME: it installs the full Claude Code integration — PreToolUse `hook rewrite` (Bash) + `hook redirect` (Read/Grep/View/ListFiles) auto-compression, observe hooks, a lean-ctx skill, a global CLAUDE.md, MCP tool permissions and the local daemon (hook commands use absolute paths to the pinned binary). The MCP stdio server (bare `lean-ctx`, 13 tools at the default profile) is declared below.
notes: Claims up to 99% savings — exactly the kind of headline THOL exists to test. Primary mechanism is passive (onboard's redirect/rewrite hooks); the MCP server adds a callable surface. The 2.1.183 arm ran an earlier, MCP-only manifest and is excluded from the board pending a re-run with this one. Validated on this machine 2026-07-10: `onboard --yes` headless in a sandbox HOME writes the hooks/skill/CLAUDE.md/permissions above; MCP handshake OK (13 tools, ~140ms); full haiku session exercised the install end-to-end.
